operating_state: Total revenue increased 7% to $113.1 billion driven by net interest income growth and noninterest income expansion. Net interest yield expanded six basis points to 2.01% from Global Markets activity, fixed-asset repricing, and deposit growth, partially offset by lower rates. Investment banking fees rose on higher debt issuance and advisory activity while investment and brokerage services increased reflecting higher market valuations and positive AUM flows. Total assets grew $142.2 billion to $3.4 trillion from commercial loan and residential mortgage growth plus trading account assets supporting client activity.
cost_structure: Noninterest expense increased to $69.7 billion from continued investments in people and technology plus higher revenue-related expenses, partially offset by reduced FDIC special assessment accrual. Efficiency ratio improved to 61.65% from 63.12% as revenue growth outpaced expense growth. Net charge-offs decreased to $1.4 billion driven by asset quality improvements across credit card and commercial portfolios, with consumer net charge-off ratio improving to 0.82% from 0.91%.
material_events: The Board authorized a $40 billion common stock repurchase program and declared quarterly dividend of $0.28 per share. The Corporation repurchased $21.4 billion of common stock and paid $8.1 billion in dividends during 2025. The Federal Reserve reduced the stress capital buffer to 2.5% from 3.2% effective October 2025. Dean Athanasia and James DeMare were appointed Co-Presidents with Alastair Borthwick elevated to Executive Vice President while retaining CFO role.
macro_exposures: The Corporation faces exposure to fluctuations in interest rates, inflation, currency exchange rates, and economic conditions including trade policies and tariff impacts. Geopolitical instability including Russia/Ukraine and Middle East conflicts present operational and market risks. Current government funding lapse and potential prolonged pause in federal functions could disrupt economic activity and increase market volatility. Adverse banking industry developments including bank failures could result in worsening economic conditions.
forward_outlook: The stress capital buffer remains 2.5% through September 2027 maintaining a CET1 minimum requirement of 10.0%. The G-SIB surcharge will increase by 50 basis points effective January 2027 increasing minimum capital requirements. Federal Reserve proposed changes to stress testing framework including enhanced disclosure and averaging recent stress test results for buffer determination. Management continues strategic investments in business capabilities with focus on technology infrastructure and client-facing capabilities.
uncertainty_profile: Pending litigation includes unemployment benefits processing, automatic credit card payment features, anti-money laundering program adequacy, and electronic payments processing including Zelle network fraud issues. Commercial real estate office exposure totaled $3.9 billion with 80% loan-to-value while HELOCs entering amortization experienced higher delinquency rates. Interest-only residential mortgages totaling $64.9 billion show higher delinquency rates upon amortization though most loans remain payment-optional until 2027 or later.
